objective: Implement the governance-owned §DA Phase 2 hybrid delegation gate so orchestrator-owned tool calls are routed away from Tier 2 specialist scope before direct execution.
scope_boundary: Add a versioned delegation rules artifact covering all §DA Delegation Rule task types.
Add deterministic delegation-gate logic with optional MCP endpoint fallback behavior and explicit confidence thresholds.
Integrate the gate into the committed governance PreToolUse hook for Agent/Task, Bash, and implementation-scoped Explore payloads while leaving Read ungated.
Add tests proving block, warn, allow, bypass, fail-open, and non-regression behavior.
Record validation, closeout, and GitHub issue evidence for issue #212.
out_of_scope: Installing or enabling the hook in user-local Claude settings.
Implementing a live Qwen classifier daemon in local-ai-machine.
Changing write-scope or planner-boundary gate semantics outside the narrow delegation-gate integration.
research_summary: Issue #212 follows §DA Phase 1 (#208/#209), which established ownership rules but left read/explore delegation enforcement soft. The current governance repo has a committed `hooks/code-write-gate.sh` PreToolUse hook, structured-plan enforcement, and execution-scope validation. This slice adds a local deterministic gate plus optional MCP URL fail-open path so downstream repos can adopt it without requiring a live classifier on day one.

sprints.name: Delegation Gate Contract
sprints.goal: Create deterministic routing rules and a reusable local gate.
tasks: Add `scripts/orchestrator/delegation_gate.py` with thresholded decisions and JSON output.
Add focused runtime tests for deterministic matching, warn-only Explore, bypass, Read allow, and classifier fallback.
acceptance_criteria: Final AC: all 8 §DA task types are represented in the rules file.
Final AC: high-confidence Agent/Bash owned-work attempts block.
Final AC: low-confidence or Read attempts allow.

sprints.name: Hook And E2E Proof
sprints.goal: Wire the gate into the governance hook and prove the issue #212 live failure shape is intercepted.
tasks: Extend `hooks/code-write-gate.sh` without removing existing governance-surface and new-code checks.
Add hook-level tests for Agent block, Explore warn, Read allow, bypass logging, and MCP timeout fail-open.
Record local and GitHub CI evidence before closing issue #212.
acceptance_criteria: Final AC: `Explore(Audit SQL migrations for schema inconsistencies)` produces a warning before direct work proceeds.
Final AC: Agent/Task or Bash equivalents for the same migration audit block and name `migration-validator`.
Final AC: configured unavailable MCP endpoint fails open to ALLOW.
Final AC: existing write-scope checks remain covered by current tests.

specialist_reviews.focus: Validate that the deterministic gate preserves §DA ownership without blocking read-only routing.
specialist_reviews.summary: Review is recorded in the issue #212 cross-review artifact before merge.

alternate_model_review.summary: Same-family review is allowed by the issue #212 exception and must run before PR publication.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Issue #212 may add delegation-gate rules, local gate code, hook integration, tests, standards text, validation, and closeout artifacts in hldpro-governance only.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Implement the deterministic gate, run local validation, obtain review, open PR, wait for green checks, and merge.

material_deviation_rules: If hook integration breaks existing write-scope behavior, revert the hook change and keep the gate as a standalone script.
If a live MCP classifier is required to satisfy acceptance, create a downstream local-ai-machine issue instead of expanding this governance slice.
If false positives hard-block Explore, downgrade Explore to warn-only and document the residual risk.
approved_by: operator instruction: follow repo rules, no HITL, e2e testing as final AC
